name: mastodon-blade-mcp
title: Mastodon
description: Mastodon operations — timelines, statuses, notifications, search, interactions, multi-instance
tagline: Browse, post, and interact across the fediverse
author: groupthink-dev

tools.name: mastodon_info
tools.description: Health check — instance URL, account, write gate status.

tools.name: mastodon_verify
tools.description: Verify credentials against the configured instance.

tools.name: mastodon_timeline_home
tools.description: Home timeline — accounts you follow. Cursor pagination. Accepts scope=public|personal|family|work mapped to Mastodon lists via MASTODON_*_LIST_ID env vars.

tools.name: mastodon_timeline_public
tools.description: Public timeline (federated or local-only).

tools.name: mastodon_timeline_local
tools.description: Local timeline of the configured instance.

tools.name: mastodon_timeline_hashtag
tools.description: Timeline filtered by a hashtag.

tools.name: mastodon_timeline_list
tools.description: Timeline of a user-defined list.

tools.name: mastodon_status
tools.description: Fetch a single status by ID.

tools.name: mastodon_context
tools.description: Replies and ancestors for a status.

tools.name: mastodon_search
tools.description: Search statuses, accounts, and hashtags. Accepts scope=public|personal|family|work to restrict status results to accounts in the scope's list.

tools.name: mastodon_account
tools.description: Fetch an account by ID or @handle.

tools.name: mastodon_account_statuses
tools.description: Statuses authored by an account. Accepts scope=public|personal|family|work as a membership precondition — refuses if account_id is outside the scope's list.

tools.name: mastodon_relationships
tools.description: Follow/mute/block relationships for one or more accounts.

tools.name: mastodon_followers
tools.description: Followers of an account.

tools.name: mastodon_following
tools.description: Accounts followed by an account.

tools.name: mastodon_notifications
tools.description: Recent notifications — mentions, follows, boosts, favourites. Accepts scope=public|personal|family|work to filter to actors in the scope's list.

tools.name: mastodon_trending_tags
tools.description: Currently trending hashtags on the instance.

tools.name: mastodon_trending_statuses
tools.description: Currently trending statuses on the instance.

tools.name: mastodon_trending_links
tools.description: Currently trending links on the instance.

tools.name: mastodon_bookmarks
tools.description: Statuses bookmarked by the authenticated account.

tools.name: mastodon_favourites
tools.description: Statuses favourited by the authenticated account.

tools.name: mastodon_lists
tools.description: User-defined lists belonging to the authenticated account.

tools.name: mastodon_list_accounts
tools.description: Accounts in a specific list.

tools.name: mastodon_conversations
tools.description: Direct-message conversations involving the authenticated account.

tools.name: mastodon_filters
tools.description: Server-side content filters configured on the account.

tools.name: mastodon_post
tools.description: Post a new status. Write — gated.

tools.name: mastodon_reply
tools.description: Reply to a status. Write — gated.

tools.name: mastodon_edit
tools.description: Edit an existing status. Write — gated.

tools.name: mastodon_delete
tools.description: Delete a status. Destructive — gated.

tools.name: mastodon_favourite
tools.description: Favourite a status. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unfavourite
tools.description: Remove a favourite. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_boost
tools.description: Boost (reblog) a status. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unboost
tools.description: Remove a boost. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_bookmark
tools.description: Bookmark a status. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unbookmark
tools.description: Remove a bookmark. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_pin
tools.description: Pin a status to the profile. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unpin
tools.description: Unpin a status from the profile. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_follow
tools.description: Follow an account. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unfollow
tools.description: Unfollow an account. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_block
tools.description: Block an account. Reversible write — destructive social impact.

tools.name: mastodon_unblock
tools.description: Unblock an account. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_mute
tools.description: Mute an account. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_unmute
tools.description: Unmute an account. Reversible write.

tools.name: mastodon_dismiss_notification
tools.description: Dismiss a single notification. Destructive.

tools.name: mastodon_media_upload
tools.description: Upload media for use in a subsequent post. Write.

env.name: MASTODON_PROVIDERS
env.description: Comma-separated provider names for multi-instance (e.g. personal,project)

env.name: MASTODON_PERSONAL_LIST_ID
env.description: Mastodon list ID mapped to scope=personal. Per-instance form: MASTODON_PERSONAL_LIST_ID_<INSTANCE>. Leave unset → scope=personal degrades to passthrough.

env.name: MASTODON_FAMILY_LIST_ID
env.description: Mastodon list ID mapped to scope=family. Per-instance form: MASTODON_FAMILY_LIST_ID_<INSTANCE>.

env.name: MASTODON_WORK_LIST_ID
env.description: Mastodon list ID mapped to scope=work. Per-instance form: MASTODON_WORK_LIST_ID_<INSTANCE>.

setup.blurb: Connect to Mastodon for timeline browsing, search, notifications, and social interactions. Multi-instance.
setup.help_text: Generate an access token at Preferences > Development > New Application on your Mastodon instance. Grant read scopes for read-only use, or read+write for full interaction.

fields.label: Access Token
fields.help: Preferences > Development > New Application > Access Token

fields.label: Enable Write Operations
fields.help: Set to 'true' to enable posting, favouriting, following, and other write operations

highlights.text: Timelines — home, local, federated, hashtag, and list feeds
Search — accounts, statuses, and hashtags across the fediverse
Notifications — mentions, boosts, favourites, follow requests
Compose — post, reply, boost, favourite, bookmark
Multi-instance — manage multiple accounts from one server
Write-gated — posting disabled by default, explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Mastodon Blade MCP gives your AI agent access to the Mastodon social network — reading timelines, searching content, managing notifications, and composing posts. It works with any Mastodon-compatible instance (Mastodon, Pleroma, GoToSocial, Akkoma) through the standard Mastodon API.

Your agent can catch up on your home timeline, search for discussions on a topic, check notifications, and draft or publish posts — all from a single conversation.

### Multi-instance support

Configure multiple accounts across different instances using `MASTODON_PROVIDERS`. Each provider gets its own instance URL and token, and your agent can switch between them within a session. Useful for managing personal and project accounts separately.

### Safety model

All write operations (posting, replying, boosting, favouriting, following) are disabled by default:

- **Read-only by default** — timelines, search, and notifications work immediately
- **Write gate** — set `MASTODON_WRITE_ENABLED=true` to unlock posting and interactions
- **Confirm gate** — each write tool requires `confirm: true` per call

This prevents accidental posts or follows while still allowing full read access for monitoring and research.

scenarios.label: Catch up on the fediverse
scenarios.body: Pull the home timeline and notifications, summarise trending discussions, and flag any mentions or replies that need a response.

scenarios.label: Research a topic across instances
scenarios.body: Search for hashtags and accounts related to a topic, review recent statuses from key accounts, and bookmark notable posts for later reference.

scenarios.label: Draft and publish a thread
scenarios.body: Compose a multi-part thread with content warnings and hashtags, preview the posts, then publish them sequentially with reply chains intact.
